text.title: Spring Boot Layered Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to produce a layered Spring Boot feature package.

step.name: design-package
step.action: Create `feature/<name>/` package with sub-packages: `web/` (controller), `app/` (service), `dto/`. Repository lives in `infra/` per `[[java-jpa-hibernate]]`.

step.name: dto-and-validation
step.action: Define request DTO as `record CreateOrderRequest(@NotBlank @Size(max=200) String customerName, @Positive BigDecimal total)`. Define response DTO as plain record. Add MapStruct mapper if entity→DTO is non-trivial.

step.name: service
step.action: `@Service` class. `@Transactional(readOnly=true)` on the class; override per write method with `@Transactional`. Method signatures take/return DTOs only. Use `Pageable` for list methods.

step.name: controller
step.action: `@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/...")`. Each method calls one service method, returns `ResponseEntity<TResponse>` or `Page<TResponse>`. `@Valid` on request DTOs. NO `@Transactional`, NO repository access.
step.decision-gate: If controller annotates `@Transactional` or autowires a repository, FAIL.

step.name: async-and-error-handler
step.action: If async: extract `@Async` methods into a separate bean; define a named `ThreadPoolTaskExecutor` bean with core/max/queue tuned for the workload. Add `@RestControllerAdvice` mapping exceptions to `ProblemDetail`.
